measured: Topology-backed backend collection and frontend list inventories
One full managed canonical timing pass with JUnit/durations
One targeted 114-node timing run for portfolio and architecture hotspots
Three temporary mutation probes without repository writes
Static AST/source/lifecycle inventory and Git history analysis
inferred: Workflow waiting/review cost where no structured timestamps exist
limitations: The one canonical pass was non-green due to existing market/provider failures; skipped/failed are not treated as passed.
No browser, UAT, release-real-runtime, or QoderWork execution was performed solely for this audit.
Vitest declarations are lexical/expanded inventory counts, not a full frontend timing pass.
Architecture category counts use documented deterministic node-name predicates.
Historical T537/T550 workflow observations were not rerun and may be stale; they are used only as process examples.

unittestSubtests.arithmetic: JUnit cases 8864 = 8142 collected parents + 722 subtest events

playwright.mechanicalDuplicationAssessment: All 357 desktop logical cases are also expanded for mobile; this is project expansion, not proven semantic redundancy.
playwright.browserBackendOverlapAssessment: Browser and backend tests share contracts but no browser case was accepted as a backend replacement.

frontendBuildBootstrap.reason: No full frontend execution or browser was required for this audit.

fixturesAndLifecycle.scopeRecommendation: Keep mutable DB, dependency overrides, auth/session state, and transport injections logically function-isolated. Cache only immutable schemas, source graphs, registries, and built artifacts under complete content identity.

rootCause: Large feature generations added unit, service, API, architecture, evidence, and browser layers without an explicit retirement owner.
Persistence phases, multi-user/RBAC, market evidence, release qualification, and frontend migrations accumulated permanent regression islands.
Topology makes ownership explicit but does not encode validation tier or retirement condition.
Parameter expansion contributes 10.955539%, so unique logic growth, not parameterization alone, is the primary size driver.

productToTestRatio.browserAttribution: Playwright topology owns cases by route/protection class, not backend production domain; per-domain browser ratios are intentionally not fabricated.
findings: Portfolio is the runtime outlier: 277 nodes consume 200.052 s despite only 20 production files.
Residual integration owns 1779 nodes and 117.127 s; governance should split surviving product owners before deletion.
Market has the largest product surface and node count, but only 51.201 s; count alone is not a cost proxy.
Auth/RBAC has high defense density that is justified by risk; optimize frequency, not semantics.

testQuality.interpretation: Heuristics rank review targets only and are not cleanup totals.
highestPriority: Whole-repository source scans with unique assertions but repeated construction.
Large parameter matrices where one root cause fans out into many failures.
Large Vitest owners that mix multiple component contracts and produce slow/opaque diffs.
Broad mocks that prove projections without exercising the authoritative owner.

failureDiagnostics.parserRisk: SUBFAILED(...) is not equivalent to parsing only 'SUBFAILED '; structured JUnit/event records are required.
diagnosticRecommendations: Emit parent outcome and child subtest events separately.
Group failures by stable owner/failure-family identity before counting new outcomes.
Retain parameter IDs for equivalence partitions; do not hide them in opaque loops.
Print live stage progress and machine-readable stage summaries.

canonicalPass.consoleSummary: 26 failed, 8097 passed, 23 skipped, 718 subtests passed in 792.82s
canonicalPass.explanation: Console counts include subtest reporting; JUnit has 26 failure elements but 23 failed parent nodes.
canonicalPass.scheduling: No concurrent T566 full suite was active. Exactly one complete timing pass ran.

targetedPass.setupTeardownObservation: The top 40 were call phases; no setup/teardown phase exceeded the 0.32 s tail shown in the targeted list.

phaseLimits.caveat: The original per-node JUnit artifact was ephemeral; aggregate phase totals and range were preserved, but exact teardown node ordering is not re-created.

semanticCoverageGraph.coverageAssessment: Necessary defense-in-depth across policy, endpoint, persistence ownership, and real browser boundaries.
semanticCoverageGraph.missingCoverage: No missing high-level boundary established by this audit.
semanticCoverageGraph.excessiveCoverage: Repeated execution frequency, not semantic ownership.

semanticCoverageGraph.contract: Provider source authority and no-live truth
semanticCoverageGraph.coverageAssessment: C03 is dominated; C10 module-specific guards remain distinct.
semanticCoverageGraph.missingCoverage: Target-environment provider qualification remains release-only and was not run.
semanticCoverageGraph.excessiveCoverage: Duplicate normalization naming and repeated static module helpers.

semanticCoverageGraph.excessiveCoverage: Large frontend owner files increase transform and triage cost.

semanticCoverageGraph.excessiveCoverage: 13 schema/service alias rows execute the same projection branch.

semanticCoverageGraph.coverageAssessment: High runtime but no deletion candidate without mutation dominance proof.
semanticCoverageGraph.excessiveCoverage: Large service matrix and repeated lifecycle, not proven redundant semantics.

repeatedValidation.reusable: yes when snapshot and complete identity match
repeatedValidation.whyRerun: evidence is not carried as an authoritative content-addressed stage record
repeatedValidation.confidenceAdded: low on identical identities; high after lock/profile changes

repeatedValidation.treeChanged: acceptance no; integration yes when combined
repeatedValidation.reusable: task-local to acceptance yes; integration must rerun if tree changes
repeatedValidation.whyRerun: trust boundary and missing signed/structured evidence
repeatedValidation.confidenceAdded: justified after combined-tree change; duplicated on unchanged SHA

repeatedValidation.whyRerun: interruption, parser ambiguity, topology reconciliation, or absent reusable evidence
repeatedValidation.confidenceAdded: none for exact identity replay; necessary after topology/tree change

repeatedValidation.whyRerun: fail-closed ownership and topology commit ordering
repeatedValidation.confidenceAdded: necessary after node changes; wasteful on unchanged inventory

repeatedValidation.whyRerun: scope expands from changed files to branch/candidate archive
repeatedValidation.quantifiedCost: release test corpus 21.287 s; scanner command cost not separately measured

overDefenseCriteria: Duplicates equivalent evidence under identical content identity.
Costs more than observed defect-detection value.
Blocks on text parser or infrastructure defects.
Makes integration tooling more complex than the integrated change.
Serializes independent work without a shared resource conflict.
Has no measured detection value or retirement/narrowing path.

stale node must be intersected with current inventory

controlAudit.control: release secret/preflight tests in backend canonical

R0.ownerTests: report arithmetic or generator check only
R0.fullTopology: only when inventory-bearing documentation or topology contract changes
R0.escalation: executable docs generator, workflow, schema, secret, or release identity change

R1.ownerTests: changed test file and retained replacement nodes
R1.fullTopology: verify affected surface; verify-all when node inventory changes
R1.escalation: shared fixture, global state, protected semantic, or gate logic change

R2.ownerTests: complete owner suite or deterministic impacted slice
R2.vitest: when web consumer or shared contract changes
R2.\u0073ecretScan: changed files plus affected generated artifacts
R2.escalation: public contract, multiple owners, persistence, provider, auth, or runtime boundary

R3.vitest: complete when shared frontend contracts change; otherwise affected

R4.scope: Auth, RBAC, persistence, provider isolation, runtime composition, migration, release identity, or other global boundary
R4.architectureTests: all relevant and global always-run guards
R4.uat: required when runtime/user-facing target behavior changes

R5.scope: Frozen release candidate or target-environment qualification
R5.ownerTests: already bound R4 evidence plus release deltas
R5.\u0073ecretScan: candidate archive, branch, and generated evidence
R5.evidenceRetention: release retention policy and rollback identity
R5.escalation: any identity mismatch invalidates evidence; rebuild candidate

suiteFrequency.recommendedFrequency: once per accepted R3 integration batch; every R4/R5 tree; scheduled/nightly for drift
suiteFrequency.alwaysTriggers: global boundary, unknown selection, topology/gate changes

suiteFrequency.recommendedFrequency: frontend integration batch, shared frontend contract, milestone, R5

suiteFrequency.alwaysTriggers: auth/session/RBAC route shell or browser harness change

suiteFrequency.recommendedFrequency: node/file inventory changes, accepted integration batch, R4/R5
suiteFrequency.notEvery: R0 docs and unchanged-inventory focused reruns

suiteFrequency.recommendedFrequency: after T630-T642 stabilization and before T643 qualification/release decision

landAndIntegration.finding: The protected logic is valid, but one-off shell orchestration repeats repository verification, allowlists, patch-id and subject checks, fetch/push retry, topology, failure inventory, and cleanup.

structuredEvidence.parserRule: Never infer parent arithmetic from one console regex. JUnit/JSON events are authority; console is display only.

resumability.current: Rerun is safer because most stages lack complete identity and journals.
resumability.reuseRule: All relevant identities must match exactly; missing or ambiguous identity fails closed and reruns.
resumability.interruptionPolicy: Reuse only atomically completed stages; incomplete stage is rerun. Never reuse mutable target-environment state without explicit snapshot identity.

safeNow: static lint/compile versus pure inventory reads
independent worktrees with separate runtime paths
owner shards proven free of shared DB/env/port state
Vitest listing and backend static analysis
stagedRollout: Stage 0: structured serial baseline and classify serial blockers.
Stage 1: shard pure/static and isolated owner suites in separate processes.
Stage 2: after T632, add portfolio/DB shards with per-worker template paths.
Stage 3: canonical parallel default only after three repeatable parity runs.
parallelism.expectedTarget: Backend canonical <=480 s, inferred; no measured xdist saving yet.

worktreeLifecycle.finding: Isolation is valuable for R3-R5 and concurrent implementation, but separate integration worktrees and full bootstrap are unnecessary for many R0/R1 changes.
worktreeLifecycle.R0: Current clean branch, changed-file checks, one commit; no dedicated integration worktree.
worktreeLifecycle.R1: Worktree only when concurrent/shared topology work; deterministic cherry-pick is sufficient.
worktreeLifecycle.R2: Isolated development worktree; integrate by deterministic fast-forward/cherry-pick after owner evidence.
worktreeLifecycle.R3_R4: Dedicated integration worktree for combined-tree evidence.
worktreeLifecycle.R5: Frozen candidate worktree/artifact; no mutation after qualification.
worktreeLifecycle.cleanup: Only after integrated/pushed identity is verified; refuse dirty, divergent, or unrecorded evidence states.

optimizedValidationOrdering: Environment/lock identity lookup using reusable verified snapshot
Syntax, formatting, static ownership, and changed-file secret scan
Focused owner tests and deterministic affected-consumer tests
Topology delta collection only when inventory may change
Canonical backend once per R3 batch or R4/R5 tree
UAT and release-real-runtime only on frozen applicable candidates
Final branch/candidate secret scan, evidence reconciliation, push, then cleanup

problems: raw node IDs break on rename/parameters
baselinePolicy.target: Stable failure-family IDs bound to owner, semantic contract, first-seen tree, current node aliases, and explicit retirement condition.
baselinePolicy.forbidden: Never coerce new failures into baseline or interpret skipped/not-evaluated as passed.

reporting.finding: Tiering removes boilerplate only for low risk; high-risk traceability is unchanged.

inferred: developer wait compounds when focused, integration, and canonical evidence are rerun
one-off LAND preparation and triage are high operator-cost
unknown: reruns attributable only to parser defects

R3.validation: affected owners then one canonical per batch
R3.expectedRuntime: <=8 min affected plus <=8 min canonical target

R4.validation: protected owners, full backend, relevant frontend/browser/UAT

protectionsNeverWeaken: missing != zero and not evaluated != passed

roadmap.rootCause: Gate decisions parse terminal text and mis-handle SUBFAILED punctuation.
roadmap.expectedRuntimeChange: No direct saving; prevents unnecessary full reruns of up to 831.673 s each.

roadmap.rootCause: Protected or unknown changes make the current fast gate fall back to universal full scope.
roadmap.expectedRuntimeChange: R0 <=2 min; R1/R2 4-6 min target; canonical unchanged.
roadmap.protectedSemantics: Fail-closed ambiguity without assigning every change to R4/R5.
roadmap.commitSubject: feat(validation): select checks by change risk

roadmap.rootCause: Portfolio nodes rebuild expensive mutable DB/cache/application state.
roadmap.protectedSemantics: Ledger atomicity, cache invalidation, owner isolation, missing != zero.

roadmap.rootCause: No xdist authority exists; global env/cache/SQLite/port state blocks naive parallelism.
roadmap.expectedRuntimeChange: Target backend <=480 s; measured benefit requires staged shard evidence.
roadmap.protectedSemantics: Deterministic isolation, no shared mutable DB/cache, no live network.

roadmap.rootCause: Three exact semantic pairs have two authorities.
roadmap.expectedRuntimeChange: 0.172 s measured plus two sub-ms calls.
roadmap.protectedSemantics: Manifest exclusion, route delegation, TickFlow non-official/non-live truth.
roadmap.rollbackBoundary: single cleanup plus topology commit policy

roadmap.rootCause: Four unique guards independently scan the same repository source graph.
roadmap.expectedRuntimeChange: 20-30 s inferred reduction from 38.49 s targeted calls.
roadmap.protectedSemantics: Every debt family remains independently named and fail-closed.

roadmap.rootCause: Schema/service parity repeats pure normalizer alias partitions.
roadmap.protectedSemantics: invalid, missing, evaluated/not-evaluated, ordering, and selected states remain distinct.

roadmap.rootCause: Release-only behavior consumes 38.65 s on every backend canonical invocation.
roadmap.expectedRuntimeChange: 38.65 s measured shift from canonical to release.
roadmap.protectedSemantics: Release and secret checks remain mandatory for relevant changes and R5.

roadmap.rootCause: Interrupted expensive stages lose completed work and rerun by default.
roadmap.expectedRuntimeChange: Avoids up to one 831.673 s rerun per valid interrupted canonical stage.
roadmap.protectedSemantics: No evidence reuse when any identity component differs.

roadmap.rootCause: Large one-off scripts repeat verification, patch-id, retry, topology, cleanup, and text parsing.
roadmap.expectedRuntimeChange: Unknown machine time; high operator-time and retry reduction.
roadmap.protectedSemantics: Idempotency, accepted-commit preservation, no premature cleanup, explicit push authority.

roadmap.rootCause: 223 raw node IDs and console matching are brittle under rename/parameterization.
roadmap.expectedRuntimeChange: No direct saving; reduces parser-driven reruns and manual reconciliation.

roadmap.rootCause: Small tasks repeat high-risk completion boilerplate.
roadmap.protectedSemantics: High-risk and release reports retain full evidence.

roadmap.rootCause: Accepted commits can wait behind unrelated work; cleanup and integration worktrees are overused for low risk.
roadmap.expectedRuntimeChange: Bootstrap and operator time reduction; not measured.
roadmap.protectedSemantics: Independent branches, verified environment, no source work loss.

roadmap.rootCause: Higher-risk consolidation requires one comprehensive stabilized-wave decision.
roadmap.expectedRuntimeChange: Qualification cost accepted; decides whether any C11 consolidation is safe.

ownerConstraints: serialize the topology update for T634

waves.dependencyConstraint: Start each task only when its roadmap dependencies resolve; T642 starts after T631.

ownerConstraints: serialize the topology update for T636

waves.dependencyConstraint: Preserve validation-authority ownership and start T639 only after T638.
T643 starts only after every implementation task T630 through T642 resolves.

rejectOrDefer.reason: identical mechanics protect distinct owners; complexity/risk exceeds benefit

rejectOrDefer.proposal: delete C11 Phase F nodes now
rejectOrDefer.reason: no mutation dominance or surviving-owner map
global-state blockers and no dependency authority

dependencyGraph.maximumSafeParallel: First parallel wave: T630, T632, T634, T641. Next dependency-ready wave: T631, T635, T642 where their dependencies permit. Then T633 and T636. Serial shared-authority work: T637, T638, T639, T640. Final comprehensive qualification: T643. Preserve all owner-conflict serialization.

retirementPrerequisites.requirement: T630 through T642 are completed, superseded, or explicitly rejected with durable decisions.

Durable risk-tier policy, validation-selection rules, parser/evidence contracts, resumability rules, and workflow ownership have moved into canonical code, configuration, generated AI manual sources, or other permanent authoritative locations.
No production code, tests, scripts, workflow, or documentation links still reference the T569 Markdown path.
Any still-required machine-readable policy has moved out of the T569 JSON.

artifactLifecycle.retirementAction: delete the T569 Markdown rather than moving it to an archive folder
artifactLifecycle.jsonRetirement: Delete the T569 JSON once no executable tooling or canonical policy depends on it; otherwise retain it only until that dependency is migrated.
artifactLifecycle.prohibitedOutcome: Do not create docs/audits/archive, historical-audits, completed-reports, or another compatibility copy.

directAnswers.whySoManyTests: Repeated feature/migration generations retained unit, service, API, architecture, evidence, browser, and release layers without retirement metadata; 90 commits since the topology baseline added 592 current nodes while 59 baseline nodes disappeared.
directAnswers.parameterExpansionProportion: 892 of 8142 nodes, 10.955539%; 7250 are unique collected callables.
directAnswers.genuinelyRedundant: Three A-grade semantic pairs are deletion-safe; thirteen scanner rows are a medium-confidence equivalence-partition reduction.
directAnswers.necessaryDefenseInDepth: Auth/RBAC, owner isolation, provider truth, persistence, release identity, browser consumer behavior, C09, and C10 protect distinct boundaries even when mechanics look similar.
directAnswers.safestImmediateReduction: T634: remove exactly C01-C03, 3 nodes, after retained-node mutation checks and topology reconciliation.
directAnswers.largestWallClockImprovement: T632 portfolio lifecycle first, followed by T635 shared source graph and staged T633 sharding; deletion alone does not materially improve wall time.
directAnswers.largestMaintenanceImprovement: T639 repository-native resumable integration/LAND primitives, enabled by T630/T638 structured evidence.
directAnswers.neverRemoveFromCanonical: Global auth/owner/provider/no-live/persistence/architecture boundary assertions and the strongest public contract owners; optimize their construction/frequency only.
directAnswers.moveToReleaseQualification: C08's 18 release secret/preflight behavior nodes, only after T630/T631/T637 preserve risk-triggered and R5 execution.
directAnswers.canParallelizeBackend: Partially, by proven owner shards after isolating env, SQLite, cache, port, singleton, and output paths. Repository-wide xdist is unsafe today.
directAnswers.parallelismBlockers: 802 env mutation sites, 743 reset/clear sites, 2469 temporary-path sites, 566 port/loopback sites, shared singletons, and no xdist dependency/config authority.
directAnswers.runtimeTargets: Fast R0 <=2 min; R1/R2 4-6 min; affected R3 <=8 min; backend canonical <=8 min; release qualification <=30 min and resumable.
directAnswers.qoderWorkTiming: Run T643 only after T630-T642 have landed, canonical outcomes are stable, and all topology/root authority conflicts are reconciled.
directAnswers.maximumSafeParallelPlan: Start T630, T632, T634, T641 concurrently; then T631/T635/T642; then T633/T636; serialize T637, T638, T639, T640; finish with T643.
directAnswers.workflowOverDefensive: Yes in frequency and scope, not in protected semantics. Universal full fallback, repeated unchanged evidence, release checks in canonical, and one-off LAND scripts are the principal excess.
directAnswers.essentialDefenses: Offline/no-live, auth/RBAC, owner isolation, provider truth, fail-closed distinctions, persistence atomicity, release identity, secrets/privacy, and target-environment qualification.
directAnswers.duplicatedDefenses: Unchanged full-gate reruns, repeated bootstrap verification, repeated topology collection, and four source-graph scans.
directAnswers.controlsTooFrequent: Full backend for low-risk work, release tooling tests in canonical, topology verify-all when inventory is unchanged.
directAnswers.controlsTooBroad: Fast-gate protected/unknown fallback and raw all-node canonical execution for R0-R2.
directAnswers.brittleControls: Console FAILED/SUBFAILED parsing, raw known-failure IDs, and large one-off shell LAND scripts.
directAnswers.largestRecentAvoidableDelay: Full non-green canonical reruns plus manual baseline/topology/parser reconciliation; one current replay costs 831.673 s before triage.
directAnswers.fullSuiteNecessity: Once per accepted R3 integration batch, every R4 combined tree, and every R5 frozen candidate; not every R0-R2 task.
directAnswers.evidenceReuse: Reuse only under exact tree/env/command/lock/config/inventory/selection/tool identity and verified artifact hashes.
directAnswers.interruptedResume: Journal atomic stages; rerun incomplete stages; reuse complete stages only under exact identity.
directAnswers.landReplacement: Repository-native idempotent verification, evidence reconciliation, LAND, status, and cleanup primitives with structured results.
directAnswers.fastestLowRiskWorkflow: Risk classify, changed-file static checks, focused owner evidence, one commit, concise report; no full canonical/browser/UAT.
directAnswers.requiredHighRiskWorkflow: Isolated worktree, protected owner/adjacent/global guards, full topology/backend, relevant frontend/browser/UAT, immutable evidence, serialized release decision.
